mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate signals easing financial conditions and a lower discount rate on equities, which lifts S&P 500 prices over the next month. The signal is confirmed when rate cuts occur after a prior plateau or rise and fail when rate increases continue or are immediately reversed by inflation shock.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is rejected if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive on average when FEDFUNDS has declined for at least two consecutive monthly observations, or if the triggered conditional hit rate is below 50% over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent trend signals labor-market deterioration and slower future consumption, which should weigh on equity prices over the next month. The effect is strongest when unemployment accelerates rather than merely stays elevated.
failure_condition: Reject if, after a UNRATE rise of more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months, SPX delivers positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of cases or the average forward return is not below unconditional average.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS and GDPC1 both accelerate, the market receives a broad demand-growth confirmation that supports higher corporate revenue expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The mechanism fails if sales growth rises without real output support.
failure_condition: Reject if joint positive changes in RSAFS and GDPC1 do not correspond to above-median 60-day SPX returns, or if the interaction term adds no predictive value relative to each series alone in rolling tests.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in INDPRO indicates stronger industrial output and cyclical earnings momentum, which should support S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is strongest when production is expanding after prior weakness.
failure_condition: Reject if INDPRO increases by more than 0.5% over 2 months but the subsequent 20-day SPX return is not positive on average, or if the signal’s hit rate falls below 45% in rolling 2-year samples.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in HOUST indicates weakening residential investment and future aggregate demand, which should translate into lower S&P 500 returns over the next month. The effect is most informative when housing starts break below their recent moving average.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST below its 6-month moving average is not followed by negative average 20-day SPX returns, or if positive returns occur in more than half of triggered observations over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising CPIAUCSL and elevated FEDFUNDS compresses equity valuation multiples through higher real-rate pressure, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next week. The signal is not active when inflation is cooling or policy is not restrictive.
failure_condition: Reject if high CPIAUCSL together with FEDFUNDS above 5% does not produce negative 5-day SPX returns on average, or if the interaction term is not negative in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A lower UNRATE combined with rising PAYEMS indicates broad labor-market strength and stable income growth, which should support S&P 500 returns over the next quarter through stronger consumption and earnings. The effect fails when employment gains are offset by a rising jobless rate.
failure_condition: Reject if joint improvements in PAYEMS and UNRATE do not correspond to positive 60-day SPX returns, or if the combined signal underperforms a simple unconditional benchmark in rolling walk-forward evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-housing-slowdown regime, an improvement in GDPC1 after weak HOUST readings marks stabilization in final demand and should lift S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal should disappear when housing remains strong and GDP acceleration is not a recovery from weakness.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 rises while HOUST remains below its prior 6-month average
failure_condition: Reject if GDP growth with subdued housing starts does not lead to above-median 20-day SPX returns, or if the conditional effect is indistinguishable from zero in any 2-year rolling window.
